name: Stir-Fried Pork on Lettuce
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pork tenderloin into 1/4-inch thick slices.
2. In a medium bowl, combine sherry, water, and soy sauce. Add the pork slices and stir to coat.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a wok or large frying pan. Add mushrooms and pea pods, and cook quickly for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring constantly.
4. Remove the vegetables and reserve them. Drain the marinade from the pork; reserve it as well.
5. Add the remaining oil to the pan. Quickly brown the pork slices for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring constantly until cooked through.
6. Stir the cornstarch into the reserved marinade. Add the vegetables and marinade back into the pan, and cook occasionally until thickened.
7. Serve the pork stir-fry over lettuce.
Servings: 4
Calories: n/a
Prep Time: 30 minutes.
